projects.title: Granadilla
projects.cardSummary: Built for schools, teachers, and students with role-based dashboards, homework workflows, and API-driven frontend experiences.
projects.overview: Granadilla is a production-deployed full-stack learning platform for schools, teachers, and students. Built with a Django REST + PostgreSQL backend and a Next.js/React frontend, it supports role-based dashboards, homework authoring, marking workflows, student submissions, and lexicon tracking.

frontend.title: Frontend
frontend.summary: Built as a Next.js/React application with API-driven UI for teacher and student workflow management.
highlights: Implemented role-based dashboards and responsive flows for authoring, submission, and marking tasks.
Used React state and REST API calls to handle assignment, class, submission, and lexicon data.
Implemented responsive CRUD operations for tasks, classroom content, and related resources.

backend.title: Backend
backend.summary: Django REST + PostgreSQL backend managing role access, school-scoped data boundaries, and secure authentication.
highlights: Implemented JWT authentication with role-based access control across user types and workflows.
Designed model relationships for users, schools, classes, assignments, and submissions.
Managed role-based access rules for different user types across core operations.
Production deployment split across Render (backend) and Vercel (frontend).
Enforced school-level data boundaries for multi-tenant usage patterns.
Combined secure auth, API-driven UI, and responsive UX in one integrated platform.

projects.title: Image Search
projects.cardSummary: A reusable React module for searching and selecting profile images from the Pexels API.
projects.overview: Image Search is a lightweight React module for searching and selecting profile images from the Pexels API, built to practice scalable frontend patterns.
highlights: Designed and implemented a reusable image search component.
Used Redux to keep interaction flows predictable across search, selection, and modal state transitions.

projects.title: Card Snap Game
projects.cardSummary: Players create or join rooms, race to call snap, and compete through timed rounds with live scoring and chat.
projects.overview: Snap is a real-time multiplayer card game built as a full-stack experience with React on the frontend and Node.js + Socket.IO on the backend to synchronize game state, player actions, and messaging across connected users.
highlights: Implemented websocket-driven room management, live chat, and synchronized round and game events.
Built interactive game UI flows for lobby, countdown, active rounds, and scoring updates.
Designed custom visual assets and interface styling for a cohesive game feel.
